meta.description: Data quality suite for the gold-zone fact_trips table. Seven expectations split into two severity tiers: CRITICAL (quarantine the partition on failure) and WARNING (emit a CloudWatch metric but continue the pipeline).
Partition must have between 1 000 and 5 000 000 rows. Fewer than 1 000 suggests a failed ingestion or empty source file. More than 5 M suggests a partition explosion or duplicate load.
Surrogate key must never be null. A null trip_id means the xxhash derivation failed upstream, making downstream joins unreliable.
pickup_date is the partition key. A null value means the row cannot be routed to a partition and will corrupt the table layout.
Pickup zone ID must be in the official TLC range 1–265. More than 1% out-of-range indicates a join key issue between trips and the zone lookup, breaking dim_location foreign key integrity.
Dropoff zone ID must be in the official TLC range 1–265. Paired with CHK-004; both fire together under the zone validity check.

Fare amount should be between $0 and $2 000. Values below 0 are refunds or data errors. Values above $2 000 are statistically implausible for NYC taxi trips and likely indicate upstream encoding issues. Threshold is 99.5% — a small number of edge cases (negotiated fares, test rows) are tolerated.

Trip duration should be between 30 seconds and 3 hours (10 800 s). Sub-30s trips are meter errors or cancelled rides. Over 3h suggests a stuck meter or timestamp bug. Threshold is 99% — airport trips and bridge traffic can legitimately push close to 3h.

cab_type must be exactly 'yellow' or 'green'. Any other value means a new cab type was added to the TLC source (e.g. FHV, HVFHV) and landed in the pipeline without a schema update. A warning lets the team add support without stopping the pipeline.
